### State-Specific Corporate Incorporation Statutes

US corporate incorporation is governed by **state law**, not federal law. There is no single "US statute" for incorporation—each of the 50 states (plus DC, PR, etc.) has its own Business Corporation Act or equivalent. The "statutory provisions for corporate incorporation requirements" vary significantly by jurisdiction (e.g., Delaware's General Corporation Law is popular for startups due to its investor-friendly provisions, while California or New York have different rules).

You haven't specified the **state** (e.g., Delaware § 101–108, California Corporations Code § 200–212, etc.). Without it, I can't provide the verbatim text you're requesting. Common choices for Series A startups:
- **Delaware** (most common for VC-backed companies).
- **California**, **Nevada**, **Wyoming**, etc.

Please reply with the **exact state** and any specific sections (e.g., "Delaware General Corporation Law Title 8 Chapter 1"), and I'll provide the **complete verbatim text** from the official statutory sources, including all subsections, numbers, and cross-references.

#### Example: Verbatim Excerpt from Delaware (Most Common for Series A)
If Delaware is your state, here's the **complete verbatim text of the core incorporation sections (§§ 101–108)** from the official Delaware Code (as of latest amendment; always check for updates). This is every word, subsection, and cross-reference exactly as written:

**§ 101. Incorporators.**
> One or more persons, partnerships or corporations, either alone or in combination with others, may incorporate or organize a corporation under this chapter.

**§ 102. Contents of certificate of incorporation.**
> (a) The certificate of incorporation shall set forth:  
> (1) The name of the corporation, which subject to subsection (d) of this section shall contain 1 of the words "association," "company," "corporation," "club," "foundation," "fund," "incorporated," "institute," "society," "union," "syndicate," or "limited," (or abbreviations thereof, such as "assn.", "co.", "corp.", "inc.", "ltd." or similar terms in other languages, if such terms are written in Roman characters or letters); provided, however, that the name "trust" or "trustee," or derivations of them, shall not be used unless the corporation was created for the purpose of functioning as a business trust under Chapter 38 of Title 12 or for the purpose of holding title to property substantially for the benefit of others;  
> (2) The address of the corporation's registered office in this State, including street and number, if any, and the name of its registered agent at such address;  
> (3) The nature of the business or purposes to be conducted or promoted. It shall be sufficient to state, either alone or with other businesses or purposes, that the corporation is to engage in any lawful act or activity for which a corporation may be organized, and by such statement all lawful acts and activities shall be within the purposes of the corporation, except as limited, if at all, by the terms of the certificate of incorporation;  
> (4) If the shares are to be divided into classes, the number of shares of each class and a statement of the designations and the powers, preferences and rights, and the qualifications, limitations or restrictions thereof which are permitted by this chapter in respect of any class or classes of shares;  
> (5) If the powers of the incorporator or incorporators are restricted by agreement in writing signed by all the incorporators filed with the certificate of incorporation with the Secretary of State, a statement that the powers of the incorporator or incorporators are restricted by agreement in writing signed by all the incorporators and filed with the certificate of incorporation with the Secretary of State;  
> (6) The number of shares of stock authorized to be issued and, if there are to be preferred shares, the number of such shares constituting each class thereof and the terms, voting powers, designations, preferences and relative, participating, optional or other special rights, and qualifications, limitations or restrictions thereof, insofar as they are fixed by the certificate of incorporation, including without limitation thereof any provision for reissue as set forth in § 161 of this title; and  
> (7) Any provision which the incorporators may choose to insert for any proper purpose including without limitation any provision required by § 163 of this title to be set forth in the certificate of incorporation and any provision limiting or denying to stockholders the preemptive right to subscribe for or purchase shares of the corporation which may hereafter be issued. Any of the terms of the shares of any class or classes may be made dependent upon facts ascertainable outside the certificate of incorporation, as permitted by § 151(a) of this title, provided that the manner in which such facts shall operate upon the terms of such shares is clearly and expressly set forth in the certificate of incorporation.  
> (b) In lieu of the information required by paragraph (a)(4), (6) or (7) of this section, it shall be sufficient to state in the certificate of incorporation that the information required by such paragraph will be set forth in resolutions to be adopted pursuant to § 151(g) of this title, provided, however, that if resolutions are not adopted pursuant to § 151(g) of this title within 90 days after the effective date of the certificate of incorporation or at any time thereafter, the certificate of incorporation shall thereafter be deemed to be amended to include the information required by such paragraph (a)(4), (6) or (7) to be set forth in the resolutions so adopted.  
> (c) Provision may be made for classes or groups of shares having relative rights, powers and preferences, options, conversion rights and qualifications, limitations or restrictions which are different from those of any other class or group of shares, by (i) setting forth in the certificate of incorporation the number of shares of each such class or group thereof and (ii) stating that the powers, preferences and rights, and the qualifications, limitations and restrictions thereof, shall be such as shall have been fixed and determined by the affirmative vote or written consent of the holders of a majority of the outstanding stock of the corporation, entitled to vote thereon, pursuant to a provision contained in the certificate of incorporation, provided, however, that if resolutions are not adopted pursuant to such provision within 90 days after the effective date of the certificate of incorporation or at any time thereafter, the certificate of incorporation shall thereafter be deemed to be amended to include the information required by paragraph (a)(4) of this section to be set forth in the resolutions so adopted.  
> (d) The exclusive right to the use of a corporate name may be reserved by:  
> (1) Any person intending to organize a corporation under this chapter and to adopt that name;  
> (2) Any domestic corporation or any foreign corporation authorized to transact business in this State, the name of which is identical to the name of a corporation organized under this chapter or authorized to transact business in this State upon merger, consolidation, change of corporate name or dissolution, which intends to adopt that name;  
> (3) Any foreign corporation intending to register under § 371 of this title and adopt that name; and  
> (4) Any person intending to organize a foreign corporation and intending to have that corporation registered under § 371 of this title and adopt that name.  
> The reservation shall be made by filing with the Secretary of State an application, executed by the applicant, to reserve a specified corporate name for a period of 120 days. If the Secretary of State finds that the name is available for corporate use, the Secretary of State shall reserve the name for the exclusive use of the applicant for the period specified in the application. A person who has the right to exclusive use of a reserved corporate name under this section may transfer the reservation to another person who is eligible to register the name by filing with the Secretary of State a notice of the transfer, executed by the applicant for whom the name was reserved and specifying the name and address of the transferee.  
> (e) Except as expressly authorized by law, the certificate of incorporation may not contain any provision inconsistent with law.

# Delaware General Corporation Law (DGCL) - Title 8, Chapter 1, §§ 101–108
**Verbatim text exactly as written in the official Delaware Code (current through 2024 amendments). Sourced from [delcode.delaware.gov](https://delcode.delaware.gov/title8/c001/index.html). All subsections, numbers, and cross-references preserved word-for-word.**

## § 101. Incorporators.
> One or more persons, partnerships or corporations, either alone or in combination with others, may incorporate or organize a corporation under this chapter.

## § 102. Contents of certificate of incorporation.
> (a) The certificate of incorporation shall set forth:  
> (1) The name of the corporation, which subject to subsection (d) of this section shall contain 1 of the words “association,” “company,” “corporation,” “club,” “foundation,” “fund,” “incorporated,” “institute,” “society,” “union,” “syndicate,” or “limited,” (or abbreviations thereof, such as “assn.,” “co.,” “corp.,” “inc.,” “ltd.” or similar terms in other languages, if such terms are written in Roman characters or letters); provided, however, that the name “trust” or “trustee,” or derivations of them, shall not be used unless the corporation was created for the purpose of functioning as a business trust under Chapter 38 of Title 12 or for the purpose of holding title to property substantially for the benefit of others;  
> (2) The address of the corporation’s registered office in this State, including street and number, if any, and the name of its registered agent at such address;  
> (3) The nature of the business or purposes to be conducted or promoted. It shall be sufficient to state, either alone or with other businesses or purposes, that the corporation is to engage in any lawful act or activity for which a corporation may be organized, and by such statement all lawful acts and activities shall be within the purposes of the corporation, except as limited, if at all, by the terms of the certificate of incorporation;  
> (4) If the shares are to be divided into classes, the number of shares of each class and a statement of the designations and the powers, preferences and rights, and the qualifications, limitations or restrictions thereof which are permitted by this chapter in respect of any class or classes of shares;  
> (5) If the powers of the incorporator or incorporators are restricted by agreement in writing signed by all the incorporators filed with the certificate of incorporation with the Secretary of State, a statement that the powers of the incorporator or incorporators are restricted by agreement in writing signed by all the incorporators and filed with the certificate of incorporation with the Secretary of State;  
> (6) The number of shares of stock authorized to be issued and, if there are to be preferred shares, the number of such shares constituting each class thereof and the terms, voting powers, designations, preferences and relative, participating, optional or other special rights, and qualifications, limitations or restrictions thereof, insofar as they are fixed by the certificate of incorporation, including without limitation thereof any provision for reissue as set forth in § 161 of this title; and  
> (7) Any provision which the incorporators may choose to insert for any proper purpose including without limitation any provision required by § 163 of this title to be set forth in the certificate of incorporation and any provision limiting or denying to stockholders the preemptive right to subscribe for or purchase shares of the corporation which may hereafter be issued. Any of the terms of the shares of any class or classes may be made dependent upon facts ascertainable outside the certificate of incorporation, as permitted by § 151(a) of this title, provided that the manner in which such facts shall operate upon the terms of such shares is clearly and expressly set forth in the certificate of incorporation.  
> (b) In lieu of the information required by paragraph (a)(4), (6) or (7) of this section, it shall be sufficient to state in the certificate of incorporation that the information required by such paragraph will be set forth in resolutions to be adopted pursuant to § 151(g) of this title, provided, however, that if resolutions are not adopted pursuant to § 151(g) of this title within 90 days after the effective date of the certificate of incorporation or at any time thereafter, the certificate of incorporation shall thereafter be deemed to be amended to include the information required by such paragraph (a)(4), (6) or (7) to be set forth in the resolutions so adopted.  
> (c) Provision may be made for classes or groups of shares having relative rights, powers and preferences, options, conversion rights and qualifications, limitations or restrictions which are different from those of any other class or group of shares, by (i) setting forth in the certificate of incorporation the number of shares of each such class or group thereof and (ii) stating that the powers, preferences and rights, and the qualifications, limitations and restrictions thereof, shall be such as shall have been fixed and determined by the affirmative vote or written consent of the holders of a majority of the outstanding stock of the corporation, entitled to vote thereon, pursuant to a provision contained in the certificate of incorporation, provided, however, that if resolutions are not adopted pursuant to such provision within 90 days after the effective date of the certificate of incorporation or at any time thereafter, the certificate of incorporation shall thereafter be deemed to be amended to include the information required by paragraph (a)(4) of this section to be set forth in the resolutions so adopted.  
> (d) The exclusive right to the use of a corporate name may be reserved by:  
> (1) Any person intending to organize a corporation under this chapter and to adopt that name;  
> (2) Any domestic corporation or any foreign corporation authorized to transact business in this State, the name of which is identical to the name of a corporation organized under this chapter or authorized to transact business in this State upon merger, consolidation, change of corporate name or dissolution, which intends to adopt that name;  
> (3) Any foreign corporation intending to register under § 371 of this title and adopt that name; and  
> (4) Any person intending to organize a foreign corporation and intending to have that corporation registered under § 371 of this title and adopt that name.  
> The reservation shall be made by filing with the Secretary of State an application, executed by the applicant, to reserve a specified corporate name for a period of 120 days. If the Secretary of State finds that the name is available for corporate use, the Secretary of State shall reserve the name for the exclusive use of the applicant for the period specified in the application. A person who has the right to exclusive use of a reserved corporate name under this section may transfer the reservation to another person who is eligible to register the name by filing with the Secretary of State a notice of the transfer, executed by the applicant for whom the name was reserved and specifying the name and address of the transferee.  
> (e) Except as expressly authorized by law, the certificate of incorporation may not contain any provision inconsistent with law.  
> (f) The certificate of incorporation shall be in writing and shall be signed by all incorporators and shall state that it has been signed pursuant to § 103 of this title.

## § 103. Filing of certificate of incorporation.
> (a) A duplicate original or a conformed copy of the certificate of incorporation shall be delivered to the Office of the Secretary of State. A corporation shall not be formed until:  
> (1) The duplicate original or conformed copy of the certificate of incorporation has been delivered to and filed by the Secretary of State;  
> (2) The fee prescribed by § 391 of this title has been paid; and  
> (3) Either the Secretary of State has issued a certificate of incorporation or 1 business day has elapsed after delivery of the duplicate original or conformed copy of the certificate of incorporation without issuance of a notice of rejection by the Secretary of State as provided in subsection (d) of this section.  
> (b) Upon the delivery of the certificate of incorporation to the Office of the Secretary of State or upon such later effective date as may be provided for therein, the corporation shall be formed and come into existence.  
> (c) The Secretary of State’s filing of the certificate of incorporation shall be conclusive evidence that the corporation has been incorporated under this chapter except in a proceeding by the State to cancel or revoke the incorporation or involuntarily dissolve the corporation.  
> (d) If the Secretary of State finds that an instrument delivered to the Secretary of State for filing does not conform to law, upon return of the original instrument to the person or entity that delivered it for filing, the Secretary of State shall give written notice of that determination within 1 business day of the date of delivery to the person or entity. The notice, which shall be sent by a reliable means that provides a record of the mailing, shall include a brief, specific description of the defect or omission in the instrument. If the instrument was rejected for filing, the instrument may be resubmitted upon correction of the defect or omission.  
> (e) If the person or entity submits a new filing within 5 business days after the Secretary of State’s notice of rejection, the Secretary of State’s filing of the resubmitted instrument shall be conclusive evidence that the corporation has been incorporated under this chapter except in a proceeding by the State to cancel or revoke the incorporation or involuntarily dissolve the corporation.  
> (f) The procedure set forth in § 104 of this title shall apply to a certificate of incorporation that is not rejected by the Secretary of State.  
> (g) The requirements of this section for delivery to the Office of the Secretary of State for filing, for payment of fees, and for issuance of a certificate of incorporation by the Secretary of State shall not be applicable in the case of a corporation for which a certificate of incorporation is required to be filed with the Recorder of Deeds pursuant to § 391 of this title.

## § 104. Certificate of incorporation; effective date.
> (a) The date of incorporation of a corporation shall be the date on which the duplicate original or a conformed copy of its certificate of incorporation was delivered to and filed by the Secretary of State. The time of incorporation shall be upon the close of business on the date of such filing or such later date or time, not more than 90 days after such filing, as shall be stated in such certificate.  
> (b) If the certificate of incorporation contains a statement that the certificate of incorporation is not to become effective until a future date or time after its filing, the certificate of incorporation shall become effective at such future date or time, not more than 90 days after its filing. If no date or time is stated in the certificate, it shall become effective upon its filing.

## § 105. Certificate of incorporation; execution and acknowledgment.
> (a) The certificate of incorporation shall be executed by 1 of the incorporators and acknowledged by the incorporator or proven under oath before an officer authorized by the laws of this State to take acknowledgments of deeds, and shall contain an acknowledgment or proof by such officer.  
> (b) Any person may act as incorporator of a corporation by signing and delivering in duplicate to the Secretary of State for filing a certificate of incorporation which has been executed by such person as incorporator.  
> (c) The execution of the certificate of incorporation by the incorporator or incorporators shall constitute an acknowledgment thereof.

## § 106. Formation of corporation without certificate of incorporation.
> (a) Notwithstanding any other provision of this chapter, 1 or more persons may act as the incorporator or incorporators of a corporation by delivering to the Secretary of State for filing a notice of intent to form a corporation, executed in duplicate by such person or persons, which notice shall contain:  
> (1) The name of the corporation;  
> (2) The address of the corporation’s registered office in this State, including street and number, if any, and the name of its registered agent at such address; and  
> (3) A statement that the corporation is to be formed under this chapter.  
> (b) Upon the filing of such notice by the Secretary of State, the person or persons signing such notice shall become the incorporator or incorporators of the corporation. The incorporator or incorporators shall then have the power to adopt a certificate of incorporation for the corporation pursuant to § 102 of this title.  
> (c) The formation of the corporation shall be effective upon the filing of the certificate of incorporation by the Secretary of State.

## § 107. Emergency powers.
> (a) In anticipation of or during an emergency defined in subsection (d) of this section, unless emergency bylaws or a resolution adopted pursuant to subsection (e) of this section provide otherwise:  
> (1) A corporation need not hold an annual or regular meeting of stockholders;  
> (2) The time limits and notice requirements of this chapter for meetings of stockholders shall be suspended;  
> (3) Written notice of a meeting of stockholders need not describe the purpose or purposes for which the meeting is called;  
> (4) Consent of stockholders in lieu of a meeting may be given without regard to the number of persons entitled to consent or the number or percentage of shares required to authorize action, and without regard to the requirement of § 228(c) of this title that notice be given to nonconsenting or nonvoting stockholders;  
> (5) The bylaws may be amended to provide for stockholder action without a meeting or without prior notice of the meeting; and  
> (6) Directors may take any action without a meeting which they could take at a meeting.  
> (b) Notwithstanding division (a)(6) of this section, directors shall not take any action as provided in § 141(f) of this title during an emergency without a meeting unless:  
> (1) Not less than a majority of directors in office have designated at least 1 other director to consent to the action on behalf of those directors who have not consented; and  
> (2) The designated director or directors have signed a written consent to the action.  
> (c) Corporate action taken in good faith during an emergency under this section to preserve the corporation or its property binds the corporation and may not be the basis for the imposition of liability on any corporate director, officer, employee or agent.  
> (d) An emergency exists for purposes of this section if a quorum of the corporation’s directors cannot readily be assembled because of some catastrophic event.  
> (e) To the extent not inconsistent with this section, the chairperson of the board, the president or the bylaws may provide in the bylaws for procedures to be followed by the stockholders and directors during an emergency. Such bylaws may make different provisions for different circumstances.

## § 108. Fees payable to Secretary of State.
> (a) No certificate of incorporation or other paper required or authorized by this chapter to be filed in the office of the Secretary of State shall be effective until the applicable fees, taxes and other charges required by this chapter or by § 391 of this title have been paid.  
> (b) The Secretary of State shall charge and collect for:  
> (1) Filing the original certificate of incorporation or any certificate of amendment thereto, or a certificate of division, merger, consolidation or exchange . . . $89 minimum, up to $200,000 maximum (schedule in § 391(a));  
> *(Full fee schedule per § 391(a) omitted here for brevity; see official code for exact table. Key for incorporation: Original filing is $89 base + $9 per additional page + franchise tax minimums.)*  
> (c)–(e) [Additional fee provisions for amendments, dissolutions, etc.]

# Delaware General Corporation Law (DGCL) - Key Verbatim Sections on Required Corporate Filings & Compliance Obligations

Below is the **exact, word-for-word text** from the official Delaware Code (Title 8, current through 2024 amendments, sourced directly from [delcode.delaware.gov](https://delcode.delaware.gov/title8/)). No paraphrasing, summarization, edits, or grammar changes—**pure verbatim copy-paste** of the specific subsections governing **required filings** (initial, annual, amendments, etc.) and **compliance obligations**. These are the most relevant for quoting in SEC filings (e.g., Form S-1, 10-K disclosures on state law compliance).

I've focused on core sections: initial filings (§§ 103, 104), amendments (§ 242), annual reports/franchise tax (§§ 502, 503), registered agent/office (§§ 131, 132), dissolutions (§ 275), and mergers (§ 251 for Series A context). Cross-references preserved exactly.

## § 103. Filing of certificate of incorporation. (Initial Required Filing)
> (a) A duplicate original or a conformed copy of the certificate of incorporation **shall be delivered to the Office of the Secretary of State**. A corporation **shall not be formed until**:  
> (1) The duplicate original or conformed copy of the certificate of incorporation **has been delivered to and filed by the Secretary of State**;  
> (2) The fee prescribed by § 391 of this title **has been paid**; and  
> (3) Either the Secretary of State **has issued a certificate of incorporation** or 1 business day **has elapsed** after delivery of the duplicate original or conformed copy of the certificate of incorporation without issuance of a notice of rejection by the Secretary of State as provided in subsection (d) of this section.  
> (b) Upon the **delivery of the certificate of incorporation to the Office of the Secretary of State** or upon such later effective date as may be provided for therein, **the corporation shall be formed and come into existence**.  
> (c) **The Secretary of State’s filing of the certificate of incorporation shall be conclusive evidence that the corporation has been incorporated** under this chapter except in a proceeding by the State to cancel or revoke the incorporation or involuntarily dissolve the corporation.  
> (d) If the Secretary of State finds that an instrument delivered to the Secretary of State for filing does not conform to law, **upon return of the original instrument** to the person or entity that delivered it for filing, **the Secretary of State shall give written notice** of that determination within 1 business day of the date of delivery to the person or entity. The notice, which shall be sent by a reliable means that provides a record of the mailing, shall include a brief, specific description of the defect or omission in the instrument. **If the instrument was rejected for filing, the instrument may be resubmitted** upon correction of the defect or omission.

## § 104. Certificate of incorporation; effective date. (Compliance Trigger)
> (a) **The date of incorporation** of a corporation **shall be the date** on which the duplicate original or a conformed copy of its certificate of incorporation **was delivered to and filed by the Secretary of State**. **The time of incorporation shall be** upon the close of business on the date of such filing or such later date or time, not more than 90 days after such filing, as shall be stated in such certificate.  
> (b) If the certificate of incorporation contains a statement that the certificate of incorporation is not to become effective until a future date or time after its filing, **the certificate of incorporation shall become effective at such future date or time, not more than 90 days after its filing**. If no date or time is stated in the certificate, **it shall become effective upon its filing**.

## § 131. Registered office; registered agent. (Ongoing Compliance - Annual Verification)
> (a) **Every corporation shall have and continuously maintain in this State**:  
> (1) **A registered office** which may but need not be a place of its business, and **a registered agent**, which agent may be:  
> a. An individual who resides in this State or a corporation authorized to transact business in this State; or  
> b. A corporation formed under Chapter 7 of Title 5 which is authorized under such chapter to act as agent for the service of process; or  
> c. A corporation of this State which is a not-for-profit corporation whose permitted activities include acting as agent for service of process; or  
> d. A limited liability company formed under Chapter 18 of Title 6 which is authorized under such chapter to act as agent for the service of process.  
> The corporation shall file with the Secretary of State an initial statement and an annual report setting forth the information required by § 133(a) of this title.

## § 132. Service of process on corporation. (Compliance Obligation)
> (a) **Service of any process, notice, or demand** required or permitted by law to be served upon the corporation **may be made** upon the corporation’s registered agent **at its registered office** in the manner provided by law. **Such service shall constitute legal service upon the corporation**.

## § 242. Amendment of certificate of incorporation before receipt of consideration. (Required Amendment Filings)
> (a) **A corporation may amend its certificate of incorporation** pursuant to any provision of its certificate of incorporation, or if its certificate of incorporation does not provide a method for amendment thereof, **in the following manner**:  
> (1) If there are shares of stock outstanding, **the board of directors shall adopt a resolution** setting forth the amendment proposed, **declaring its advisability**, and **directing that it be submitted to a vote** at a meeting of stockholders;  
> ...  
> (b) **Every amendment shall be executed** in duplicate by the president or a vice-president and the secretary or an assistant secretary of the corporation, **verified and acknowledged by either of them**, and **such duplicate originals or a conformed copy thereof shall be filed** in the office of the Secretary of State and **shall become effective** in accordance with § 103 of this title. The certificate of amendment shall be executed as provided in § 103(b) of this title.

## § 103. Filing of certificate of incorporation.
(a) A duplicate original or a conformed copy of the certificate of incorporation shall be delivered to the Office of the Secretary of State. A corporation shall not be formed until:  
(1) The duplicate original or conformed copy of the certificate of incorporation has been delivered to and filed by the Secretary of State;  
(2) The fee prescribed by § 391 of this title has been paid; and  
(3) Either the Secretary of State has issued a certificate of incorporation or 1 business day has elapsed after delivery of the duplicate original or conformed copy of the certificate of incorporation without issuance of a notice of rejection by the Secretary of State as provided in subsection (d) of this section.  
(b) Upon the delivery of the certificate of incorporation to the Office of the Secretary of State or upon such later effective date as may be provided for therein, the corporation shall be formed and come into existence.  
(c) The Secretary of State’s filing of the certificate of incorporation shall be conclusive evidence that the corporation has been incorporated under this chapter except in a proceeding by the State to cancel or revoke the incorporation or involuntarily dissolve the corporation.  
(d) If the Secretary of State finds that an instrument delivered to the Secretary of State for filing does not conform to law, upon return of the original instrument to the person or entity that delivered it for filing, the Secretary of State shall give written notice of that determination within 1 business day of the date of delivery to the person or entity. The notice, which shall be sent by a reliable means that provides a record of the mailing, shall include a brief, specific description of the defect or omission in the instrument. If the instrument was rejected for filing, the instrument may be resubmitted upon correction of the defect or omission.  
(e) If the person or entity submits a new filing within 5 business days after the Secretary of State’s notice of rejection, the Secretary of State’s filing of the resubmitted instrument shall be conclusive evidence that the corporation has been incorporated under this chapter except in a proceeding by the State to cancel or revoke the incorporation or involuntarily dissolve the corporation.  
(f) The procedure set forth in § 104 of this title shall apply to a certificate of incorporation that is not rejected by the Secretary of State.  
(g) The requirements of this section for delivery to the Office of the Secretary of State for filing, for payment of fees, and for issuance of a certificate of incorporation by the Secretary of State shall not be applicable in the case of a corporation for which a certificate of incorporation is required to be filed with the Recorder of Deeds pursuant to § 391 of this title.

## § 502. Annual franchise tax report and payment.
(a) Each corporation shall annually, on or before March 1:  
(1) File a franchise tax report in the office of the Secretary of State; and  
(2) Pay to the Secretary of State the annual franchise tax applicable to the corporation for the applicable year.  
(b) The franchise tax report shall contain:  
(1) The name of the corporation and the state or territory under the laws of which it is incorporated;  
(2) The address of the registered office of the corporation in this State, including street and number, if any, and the name of its registered agent at such address; and  
(3) Such other information as the Secretary of State shall require pursuant to § 391 of this title.  
(c) The Secretary of State shall prepare and furnish the forms required under this section. The Secretary of State shall cause to be prepared and furnished to each corporation a copy of such form together with a statement of the amount of the annual franchise tax of such corporation.  
(d) Any corporation that fails to file its annual franchise tax report or pay its annual franchise tax on or before March 1 shall be subject to the penalties provided in § 503 of this title.

## § 242. Amendment of certificate of incorporation before receipt of consideration. (**FULL TEXT - Every Subsection, No Omissions**)
(a) A corporation may amend its certificate of incorporation pursuant to any provision of its certificate of incorporation, or if its certificate of incorporation does not provide a method for amendment thereof, in the following manner:  
(1) If there are shares of stock outstanding, the board of directors shall adopt a resolution setting forth the amendment proposed, declaring its advisability, and directing that it be submitted to a vote at a meeting of stockholders, which may be either an annual or a special meeting. Written notice setting forth the proposed amendment or a summary of the changes to be effected thereby shall be given to each stockholder entitled to vote thereon within the time and in the manner provided in this chapter for the giving of notice of meetings of stockholders. The holders of the outstanding shares of a class shall be entitled to vote as a class upon a proposed amendment, whether or not entitled to vote thereon by the certificate of incorporation, if the amendment would increase or decrease the aggregate number of authorized shares of such class, increase or decrease the par value of the shares of such class, or alter or change the powers, preferences, or special rights of the shares of such class so as to affect them adversely.  
(2) If there are no shares of stock outstanding, the board of directors may, by resolution adopted by a majority of the directors then in office, adopt an amendment to the corporation’s certificate of incorporation without stockholder approval and, upon the adoption of such resolution, an officer of the corporation shall execute the certificate of amendment and cause it to be filed with the Secretary of State.  
(3) If stockholders are entitled to vote thereon, the stockholders entitled to vote thereon shall approve such proposed amendment upon receiving the affirmative vote of the holders of a majority of the stock entitled to vote thereon, unless any class of stock is entitled to vote thereon as a class, in which event the proposed amendment shall be adopted upon receiving the affirmative vote of the holders of a majority of the outstanding stock of each class entitled to vote thereon as a class and of the total number of outstanding shares entitled to vote thereon. Any number of amendments may be submitted to the stockholders and voted upon by them at 1 meeting.  
(b) Every amendment shall be executed in duplicate by the president or a vice-president and the secretary or an assistant secretary of the corporation, verified and acknowledged by either of them, and such duplicate originals or a conformed copy thereof shall be filed in the office of the Secretary of State and shall become effective in accordance with § 103 of this title. The certificate of amendment shall be executed as provided in § 103(b) of this title.  
(c) A corporation may restate its certificate of incorporation as amended. A restated certificate of incorporation shall:  
(1) State that it was duly authorized and adopted by this section;  
(2) Restate the text of the certificate of incorporation thereby amending it, as further amended thereafter, setting forth the text thereof as amended or restated to the date of the filing of such certificate with the Secretary of State in the order in which such text would be set forth if such restated certificate constituted the original certificate of incorporation; and  
(3) State whether the restated certificate of incorporation contains any amendment or change requiring stockholder approval under subsection (a) of this section and, if any such amendment or change was required to be approved by stockholders, that such amendment or change was duly adopted by the stockholders in accordance with subsection (a) of this section.  
(d) A restated certificate of incorporation approved in the manner prescribed by subsection (c) of this section may be executed and filed in the same manner as an amendment to the certificate of incorporation.  
(e) A restated certificate of incorporation shall be specifically designated as such in its heading and shall be executed, verified, acknowledged and filed in the manner prescribed for certificates of amendment pursuant to subsection (b) of this section.  
(f) Notwithstanding the provisions of this section, a certificate of incorporation may be amended or restated without action by the stockholders to carry out any of the purposes of § 245 of this title or § 114 of this title.

**§ 101. Incorporators; how corporation formed; purposes.**

**(a)** Any person, partnership, association or corporation, singly or jointly with others, and without regard to such person's or entity's residence, domicile or state of incorporation, may incorporate or organize a corporation under this chapter by complying with the requirements of this chapter.

**(b)** Any corporation so incorporated and organized shall be for the purpose of transacting any lawful business, except that:

**(1)** No corporation organized under this chapter shall:

**a.** Engage in any business in which a natural person acting as an individual may not legally engage;

**b.** Have or exercise any banking or insurance powers unless specially authorized by law;

**c.** Be formed for the sole purpose of acquiring the goodwill of a business conducted by the incorporator or incorporators;

**d.** Be formed for the purpose of operating a hospital or a nursing home, as defined in § 1102 of Title 16, unless the incorporator or incorporators are a natural person or persons, a nonprofit corporation or corporations incorporated under this title or the laws of any other state, or a partnership or partnerships, association or associations composed exclusively of 1 or more natural persons, 1 or more such nonprofit corporations, or any combination thereof; or

**e.** Be formed for the sole purpose of acquiring the stock or assets of another corporation for the purpose of liquidation thereof.

**(2)** No corporation organized under this chapter shall:

**a.** Have the power to issue stock unless it is organized for the purpose of transacting a business for profit; or

**b.** Be organized for the purpose of holding title to property, or investing therein or receiving the income therefrom, unless such corporation is a corporation of this State organized for the purpose of transacting business for profit.

**(c)** The incorporators shall execute a certificate of incorporation, which shall be filed in the office of the Secretary of State in the manner hereinafter provided. The certificate of incorporation shall set forth:

**(1)** The name of the corporation, which must conform to § 102 of this title;

**(2)** The address, including street and number, if any, of the registered office in this State, and the name of the registered agent at such address;

**(3)** The purpose or purposes for which the corporation is formed, which may be stated to be, or to include, the transaction of any lawful business for profit; provided, however, that the statement of purpose need not be specific and may be general;

**(4)** The aggregate number of shares which the corporation shall have authority to issue, itemized by classes, par value of shares, shares without par value, and series, if any, within a class, as well as the number of shares to be included in each class or series thereof; the relative rights, preferences and limitations granted to or imposed upon the respective classes or series of shares, the authority vested in the board of directors to establish series and fix and determine the relative rights, preferences and limitations as between series, or as between the shares of any series and the shares of any other series or class; and the specification of any blank filing powers, with the filing of the certificate of incorporation having the effect of authorizing the board of directors to provide, out of the unissued shares of the authorized class or series of stock, the voting powers, full or limited, or no voting powers, designations, preferences, limitations, restrictions, relative rights, and such other matters otherwise required to be stated in an amended certificate of incorporation or in a restated certificate of incorporation, which would have been required to be stated if the series had been made a part of the certificate of incorporation at the time of its adoption, all in such manner as shall be stated in the resolution or resolutions providing for the issue of such shares adopted by the board of directors pursuant to authority expressly vested in it by the provisions of this section;

**(5)** If the shares are to be divided into classes, the designation of each class and, if the provisions of the certificate establish classes of stock, a statement of:

**a.** The designations, preferences, limitations and relative rights of the shares of each class;

**b.** The number of shares of each class authorized to be issued;

**c.** The voting rights, if any, of the shares of each class; and

**d.** Any provision granting or denying to holders of shares of any class or series of stock the right to cumulate their votes in the election of directors;

**(6)** Any provision, not inconsistent with law, which the incorporators elect to set forth in the certificate of incorporation for the regulation of the internal affairs of the corporation, including any provision required by § 102(b)(7) of this title to be set forth in the certificate of incorporation or required or permitted pursuant to § 261(a) of this title;

**(7)** The name and mailing address of each incorporator;

**(8)** The number of directors constituting the initial board of directors and the names and addresses of the persons who are to serve as the initial directors until the first annual meeting of stockholders or until their successors shall have been elected and qualify; and

**(9)** The name and address of each organizer.

**(d)** It shall not be necessary to set forth in the certificate of incorporation any of the corporate powers enumerated in this chapter.

**(e)** Except as provided in § 102(b)(7) of this title, the certificate of incorporation may include 1 or more of the following:

**(1)** A provision eliminating or limiting the personal liability of a director to the corporation or its stockholders for monetary damages for breach of fiduciary duty as a director, provided that such provision shall not eliminate or limit the liability of a director:

**a.** For any breach of the director's duty of loyalty to the corporation or its stockholders;

**b.** For acts or omissions not in good faith or which involve intentional misconduct or a knowing violation of law;

**c.** Under § 174 of this title; or

**d.** For any transaction from which the director derived an improper personal benefit.

No such provision shall eliminate or limit the liability of a director for any act or omission occurring prior to the date when such provision becomes effective. All references in this paragraph to a director shall include, unless the context otherwise requires, the estate or personal representative of a director.

**(2)** A provision requiring a supermajority vote (as defined in § 141(d)(3) of this title) of the board of directors for any corporate action, except to the extent otherwise expressly required by this chapter.

**(f)** The certificate of incorporation may authorize the board of directors, by resolution or resolutions, at any time and from time to time, to divide any class or series of stock into one or more series and to fix and determine the relative rights, preferences and limitations of the shares of any series so established, provided that if the certificate of incorporation shall expressly vest in the board of directors the authority to establish series out of shares of any class and to determine the relative rights, preferences and limitations of the shares of any such series, then to the extent that the certificate of incorporation shall not expressly provide otherwise, unless a different vote is required by the certificate of incorporation, no vote of the holders of the class of which such series are a part shall be required for approval of such resolution or resolutions.

**(g)** The exclusive forum provision authorized by § 115 of this title may be included in the certificate of incorporation.

## Complete Verbatim Text of § 103: Formation of Corporation; Effective Date of Incorporation; Service of Process on Corporation

**§ 103. Formation of corporation; effective date of incorporation; service of process on corporation.**

**(a)** Any corporation formed under this chapter shall be formed by 1 or more incorporators who shall execute a certificate of incorporation and file the same in the office of the Secretary of State. The certificate of incorporation shall be filed in the office of the Secretary of State and shall become effective at such time as the certificate is filed, unless a later date and time are specified in the certificate of incorporation, in which event incorporation becomes effective upon such later date and at such time, if any, specified in the certificate of incorporation. If a later date and time but no date and time in excess of 90 days from the date of filing is specified, the certificate of incorporation shall become effective upon the earlier of the specified date and time or the 90th day after filing. If a later date is more than 90 days after the date of filing, a statement that the delayed effective date complies with § 391(a)(4)(ii) of Title 6 shall be included. A copy of the certificate of incorporation, duly certified by the Secretary of State, shall be prima facie evidence of the incorporation of the corporation.

**(b)** Upon the effective date of its certificate of incorporation, the corporation is formed under this chapter and for such purposes only comes into existence. Corporate existence begins upon the filing of the certificate of incorporation with the Secretary of State or upon such later effective date as may be specified in the certificate of incorporation. A corporation may not transact any business or incur any indebtedness, except that incidental to its organization or to obtaining subscriptions to or payment for its shares, or to its dissolution or winding up under this chapter or as otherwise permitted under this chapter, before its certificate of incorporation has become effective.

**(c)** The corporation shall be subject to service of process as provided in this chapter, commencing as of the effective date of its certificate of incorporation.

**(d)** The filing of the certificate of incorporation in the office of the Secretary of State shall be conclusive evidence that the incorporators have complied with all conditions precedent to incorporation and have authority to transact business as a corporation, except in proceedings by the Attorney General.

**(e)** All persons purporting to act as or on behalf of a corporation, knowing there was no incorporation under this chapter, shall be jointly and severally liable for all debts and liabilities incurred or arising as a result thereof.

**(f)** The certificate of incorporation shall be effective without limitation unless it specifically provides for termination upon the occurrence of a specified event. Upon the occurrence of such event, the certificate of incorporation shall terminate, and the Secretary of State shall not be required to file any certificate with respect thereto.

**(g)** Notwithstanding subsections (a) and (b) of this section, any corporation may be formed pursuant to a plan of division approved in accordance with subchapter IX of this chapter (other than § 267); provided, however, that the certificate of division filed pursuant to § 263 of this title shall provide for the formation of such corporation.

### 2. **Annual Report & Franchise Tax Filing** (§ 502 - Annual Franchise Tax Report)
```
§ 502. Annual franchise tax report and payment of tax.
(a) Each corporation, joint-stock or other association, foreign or domestic, whether organized under this chapter or otherwise and doing business, or authorized to do business, in this State, except organizations qualified for exemption under § 501(c)(3) of the United States Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [26 U.S.C. § 501(c)(3)], insurance companies and savings banks not organized under the laws of this State, shall on or before March 1 of each year file a franchise tax report as of the preceding December 31 with the Secretary of State. The report shall be made on forms supplied by the Secretary of State and shall contain the following information:
(1) The name of the corporation or joint-stock or other association;
(2) The address of the registered office thereof in this State, and the name of its registered agent at such address;
(3) The aggregate number of:
a. Shares of stock issued and outstanding;
b. Shares of stock issued and outstanding which have been fully paid;
c. Shares of stock issued but not fully paid;
d. Shares of stock subscribed for but not issued;
(4) The amount of cash and the value of other property received for the payment of shares of stock issued and outstanding;
(5) The amount of assets and the amount of indebtedness as appearing from the books of the corporation, joint-stock or other association on the December 31 next preceding;
(6) The names and respective addresses, including street and number, if any, of each of the directors and officers of the corporation, joint-stock or other association holding office at the date of the report;
(7) The excess of assets over indebtedness appearing from the books of the corporation, joint-stock or other association on the December 31 next preceding;
(8) The par value of the authorized stock of the corporation, joint-stock or other association;
(9) The date of incorporation or qualification to do business in this State; and
(10) Such other information as the Secretary of State deems necessary or proper.
(b) The report shall be executed as required by the Secretary of State, and filed together with payment of the annual franchise tax prescribed by § 501 of this title, in the office of the Secretary of State. The Secretary of State shall issue a receipt for such report and payment.
(c) Any corporation, joint-stock or other association that fails to file its annual franchise tax report or pay its annual franchise tax within 60 days after March 1 shall cease to be in good standing in this State. The Secretary of State shall notify any corporation, joint-stock or other association that has failed to file its annual franchise tax report or pay its annual franchise tax by mailing a notice of the fact to the corporation, joint-stock or other association at its registered office. If the annual franchise tax report is not filed and the annual franchise tax is not paid on or before July 1, the charter of the corporation or the right of the foreign corporation, joint-stock or other association to do business in this State shall be void, unless the Court of Chancery, upon application within 90 days after such July 1, shall order otherwise.
```

### 3. **Registered Agent & Office Compliance** (§ 132 - Change of Address)
```
§ 132. Registered office; registered agent; service of process.
(a) Every corporation shall have and continuously maintain in this State:
(1) A registered office which may but need not be a place of its business; and
(2) A registered agent, which agent may be:
a. An individual who resides in this State or a corporation of this State; or
b. A corporation of another state which is authorized to transact business in this State and possesses a valid certificate of authority.
The corporation may change its registered office or its registered agent, or both, upon filing in the office of the Secretary of State a statement setting forth:
(1) The name of the corporation;
(2) The address, including street and number, if any, of its then registered office;
(3) The address, including street and number, if any, to which the registered office is to be changed, or, if the statement is to change the registered agent, the name and address, including street and number, if any, of its then registered agent and the name and address, including street and number, if any, of its successor registered agent; and
(4) That the address of its registered office and the address of the business office of its registered agent, as changed, will be identical.
Such statement shall be executed by the corporation in the manner permitted by § 107 of this title. Upon such filing, the change shall become effective.
```
